Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Tony Hoare has died 1135 upvotes, 155 comments
  2. Defeat as Method 28 upvotes, 3 comments
  3. HyperCard discovery: Neuromancer, Count Zero, Mona Lisa Overdrive (2022) 47 upvotes, 10 comments
  4. Show HN: How I Topped the HuggingFace Open LLM Leaderboard on Two Gaming GPUs 208 upvotes, 73 comments
  5. I used pulsar detection techniques to turn a phone into a watch timegrapher 52 upvotes, 14 comments
  6. Intel Demos Chip to Compute with Encrypted Data 190 upvotes, 71 comments
  7. Open Weights isn't Open Training 54 upvotes, 16 comments
  8. The Gervais Principle, or the Office According to “The Office” (2009) 261 upvotes, 115 comments
  9. Exploring the ocean with Raspberry Pi–powered marine robots 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  10. Launch HN: RunAnywhere (YC W26) – Faster AI Inference on Apple Silicon 148 upvotes, 62 comments
  11. I put my whole life into a single database 378 upvotes, 184 comments
  12. We are building data breach machines and nobody cares 52 upvotes, 19 comments
  13. Surpassing vLLM with a Generated Inference Stack 23 upvotes, 6 comments
  14. FFmpeg-over-IP – Connect to remote FFmpeg servers 47 upvotes, 19 comments
  15. Online age-verification tools for child safety are surveilling adults 437 upvotes, 264 comments
  16. Levels of Agentic Engineering 54 upvotes, 27 comments
  17. Meta acquires Moltbook 305 upvotes, 205 comments
  18. Billion-Parameter Theories 73 upvotes, 42 comments
  19. Because Algospeak 17 upvotes, 6 comments
  20. Rebasing in Magit 159 upvotes, 109 comments
  21. Debian decides not to decide on AI-generated contributions 223 upvotes, 175 comments
  22. Agents that run while I sleep 102 upvotes, 74 comments
  23. RISC-V Is Sloooow 39 upvotes, 24 comments
  24. Redox OS has adopted a Certificate of Origin policy and a strict no-LLM policy 334 upvotes, 339 comments
  25. Roblox is minting teen millionaires 17 upvotes, 10 comments
  26. Launch HN: Didit (YC W26) – Stripe for Identity Verification 44 upvotes, 44 comments
  27. Widevine retiring its Cloud License Service (CLS) 32 upvotes, 34 comments
  28. Yann LeCun raises $1B to build AI that understands the physical world 185 upvotes, 274 comments
  29. I built a programming language using Claude Code 78 upvotes, 115 comments
  30. After outages, Amazon to make senior engineers sign off on AI-assisted changes 69 upvotes, 218 comments

Last generated 5:00am, 11 March 2026. Source here.
